scope: Performance Analyst + Structure Critic + Config Auditor
context: Phase 3 review after 2 iterations of code fixes. Searching for NEW issues only.

findings.title: KPI PUT endpoint still fetches ALL creators without limit at scale
findings.code_snippet:
const [creators, samples, outreach, latestKpi] = await Promise.all([
    supabase.from('creators').select('tier, status, source, monthly_gmv', { count: 'exact' }),
findings.trace: PUT /api/kpi → supabase.from('creators').select() → NO LIMIT clause → reads entire table into memory
findings.scenario: At 30K creators, this query loads 30,000 creator rows into memory, then filters in JS with 6 separate .filter() loops. With 8 columns per row at ~100 bytes each, that's ~24MB per request. At 10 requests/min during peak hours, peak memory usage = ~24MB * 10 concurrent = ~240MB for this endpoint alone.
findings.impact: Memory exhaustion, cold-start timeout (serverless), pagination degradation, N+1 filtering overhead.
findings.root_cause: Analyzer in previous iteration added limit(30) to content_tracking and limit(500) to computeMetrics, but missed PUT endpoint which is high-traffic read.
findings.fix: Add .limit(500) clause + move filtering to database SELECT clause using multiple eq() + in() filters instead of JS filtering. Alternatively, use RPC function to aggregate counts in one query.

findings.title: N+1 in referral status updates: one query per referral to check creator + content
findings.trace: updateReferralStatuses() → for loop over referrals → 2 DB queries per referral (creator lookup + content check)
findings.scenario: 1,000 pending referrals → 2,000 sequential DB round-trips. At 100ms per query, that's 200 seconds total time. Even with Supabase connection pooling, this will timeout at 60s (Lambda max).
findings.impact: Cron job timeout, missed status updates, incomplete bonus calculations, referral lifecycle stalled.
findings.root_cause: Referral engine designed before batch optimization. Should use batch lookup or JOIN.
findings.fix: Rewrite as two batch lookups: (1) Join referrals → creators on tiktok_handle, fetch all in one query; (2) Batch select content_tracking rows for all creator_ids. Use Promise.all() to parallelize the two lookups.

findings.title: Apify crawler polling waits 5 seconds per iteration, may block other sync jobs
findings.trace: ApifyTikTokCrawlerAdapter.waitAndGetResults() → polling loop → 5s sleep per iteration (default 300s max wait = ~60 iterations)
findings.scenario: One Apify job takes 60 seconds (12 iterations × 5s). Sync job orchestrator runs syncProfiles and syncContent in parallel, but crawler adapter blocks on each. If 10 creators need profile fetch and each takes 60s polling, total = 60s (not 600s due to concurrency), but blocks syncContent from starting until first batch completes.
findings.impact: Longer overall sync window, tight cron schedule, may miss secondary jobs (shop orders).
findings.root_cause: Conservative polling interval chosen for stability. Should be adaptive (exponential backoff or webhook).
findings.fix: Reduce polling interval to 1-2s for first 10 iterations, then 5s. Or implement Apify webhook callback to remove polling altogether.

findings.title: content_tracking table schema has NO updated_at column but sync-orchestrator writes updated_at
findings.trace: schema.sql creates content_tracking without updated_at → sync-orchestrator.ts line 162 tries to upsert with updated_at → Supabase returns 'column updated_at does not exist' error.
findings.scenario: syncContent() upsert fails silently or throws unhandled error. Videos are not inserted. Content metrics remain stale. After 7 days, all creators show no recent activity.
findings.impact: Content tracking fails completely, recommendation engine has no data, engagement metrics are wrong.
findings.root_cause: Schema was defined in v1, but codebase evolved in v2/v3 with migrations that were never applied. Trigger for updated_at exists for other tables (schema.sql:307-323) but not content_tracking.
findings.fix: Add updated_at column to content_tracking in schema-v3 with trigger, OR remove updated_at write from sync-orchestrator.ts upsert and only use posted_at/created_at. Recommend option 1 for auditability.

findings.title: referral_codes and referrals tables missing updated_at trigger despite schema-v2 RPC referencing it
findings.trace: schema-v2 defines RPC increment_referral_code_uses with updated_at = NOW(), schema-v3 creates referral_codes without updated_at column, RPC fails when called.
findings.scenario: processReferralSignup() calls increment_referral_code_uses() RPC (line 148) → SQL error 'column updated_at does not exist'. Bonus not awarded. Referral tracking breaks.
findings.impact: Referral code usage never incremented, referral lifetime tracking broken, analytics show no usage patterns.
findings.root_cause: Migrations not applied in correct order. Schema-v2 defined RPC expecting v3 schema, but v3 doesn't include updated_at columns.
findings.fix: Add updated_at column to both referral_codes and referrals tables in schema-v3 with auto-update trigger. Verify RPC compiles after migration.

findings.title: Multiple schema files (v1, v2, v3) with no version tracking or migration manifest
findings.location: supabase/schema.sql, schema-v2.sql, schema-v3.sql with no supabase/migrations/ folder
findings.code_snippet: Three separate .sql files, each complete schema snapshots. No ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN. No created_at timestamp on schema versions. No applied_at tracking in DB.
findings.trace: Developer must manually decide which schemas to apply. If v2 is applied twice, constraints break. If v3 is applied before v2, RPC references non-existent columns. No rollback path.
findings.scenario: Team member pushes new code that requires schema-v3. Another team member's local branch still has v2. Merge conflict in schema.sql. They apply v2 AND v3 manually. duplicate table errors. Supabase sync fails.
findings.impact: Schema sync confusion, inconsistent DB state across environments, hard to debug which migrations applied.
findings.root_cause: Supabase migrations folder not initialized. Schema versioning done ad-hoc.
findings.fix: Use supabase/migrations/ folder with timestamped files (001_initial_schema.sql, 002_add_creator_accounts.sql, 003_add_referral_system.sql). Use `supabase db push` which generates migrations automatically.

findings.title: Cron schedule (UTC) hardcoded in vercel.json but GET /api/sync compares against UTC hour with no timezone offset
findings.trace: Vercel Cron fires at UTC 3:00 AM → triggers GET /api/sync → code checks if new Date().getUTCHours() === 3 → TRUE → runs full sync. But comments in code say '새벽 3AM' (dawn 3AM Korea), which is UTC 6PM previous day.
findings.scenario: Team assumes sync runs at 3 AM Korea time (UTC+9, so UTC 6 PM). But vercel.json cron is UTC 3 AM (9 AM Korea time). Full sync runs during peak user hours, causing server load spike instead of off-hours. Comments are misleading.
findings.impact: Wrong sync timing, peak-hour load, slow dashboard responses, incorrect KPI snapshots captured at wrong time.
findings.root_cause: Cron schedule in vercel.json and API logic both hardcoded to UTC without timezone comment. Internationalization (Korea time) assumed in comments but not code.
findings.fix: Clarify intent: (1) If off-hours sync desired, cron schedule should be '0 18 * * *' (UTC 6 PM = 3 AM Korea). (2) Add explicit timezone comment in vercel.json and code. (3) Use environment variable CRON_TIMEZONE to allow configuring per-environment.

findings.title: package.json has zod but only /api/join uses it; other POST endpoints are unvalidated
findings.trace: Zod installed but unused in 4+ POST/PATCH endpoints. Developers have access to validation tool but don't know it's there. POST bodies sent directly to DB insert().
findings.scenario: Attacker POSTs to /api/samples with extra fields: {shipment_id: '123', status: 'delivered', updated_at: '2000-01-01', admin_override: true}. Supabase RLS allows authenticated users full CRUD (schema.sql:365-366), so admin_override might somehow propagate to RLS context (unlikely but untested).
findings.impact: Potential mass assignment vulnerability, inconsistent data types in DB, missing required field validation.
findings.root_cause: Zod setup for /api/join as example, but no team convention established. Developers unaware of validation requirement.
findings.fix: Add zod schema to all POST/PATCH endpoints. Create src/lib/validation.ts with reusable schemas (createSampleSchema, updateSampleSchema, etc.). Add lint rule to flag endpoints without schema validation.

findings.title: .env.example lists ANTHROPIC_MODEL_ID but code uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; inconsistent naming
findings.code_snippet:
.env.example:
  # AI Recommendations (optional — falls back to rule-based)
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-anthropic-key

src/lib/ai/prompt-templates.ts (line 5):
  model: process.env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL_ID || 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',

src/lib/ai/recommendation-engine.ts (line 297):
  const apiKey = process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY;
findings.trace: .env.example documents ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, but code reads ANTHROPIC_MODEL_ID (separate var). New dev sets ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=xxx in .env, forgets ANTHROPIC_MODEL_ID, code falls back to hardcoded model.
findings.scenario: Developer wants to test with claude-opus-4 but doesn't see ANTHROPIC_MODEL_ID in .env.example. Assumes code defaults to a model. Deploys to prod. Prod silently uses claude-sonnet-4-20250514 instead of expected claude-opus-4. Slow recommendations, cost mismatch.
findings.impact: Undocumented environment variable, wrong model at runtime, cost/latency surprises.
findings.root_cause: Variable name mismatch between old anthropic setup (before AI_CONFIG consolidation) and new setup.
findings.fix: Update .env.example to include ANTHROPIC_MODEL_ID with default value. Add comment: 'ANTHROPIC_MODEL_ID overrides default model in AI_CONFIG. Leave blank to use claude-sonnet-4-20250514.'

findings.title: tsconfig isolatedModules=true but barrel exports use non-type exports
findings.code_snippet:
tsconfig.json:
  "isolatedModules": true,

src/lib/adapters/index.ts:
  export { TikTokShopAdapter } from './tiktok-shop.adapter';  // Class export
  export { ApifyTikTokCrawlerAdapter } from './tiktok-crawler.adapter';  // Class export
  export { DataSyncOrchestrator } from './sync-orchestrator';  // Class export
findings.trace: isolatedModules forces each file to compile independently without cross-file type analysis. Barrel exports of classes are fine. But if code later uses `import type { TikTokShopAdapter }` from a barrel that exports value, TypeScript may produce incorrect .d.ts files.
findings.scenario: Developer imports: `import type { DataSyncOrchestrator } from '@/lib/adapters'` expecting type-only import to be tree-shaken. But DataSyncOrchestrator is exported as value (class), not `export type`. With isolatedModules, tsc doesn't guarantee removal of unused class from bundle.
findings.impact: Potential bundle bloat if consuming code uses type-only imports from value-exporting barrels.
findings.root_cause: isolatedModules is strict mode, but barrels mix value and type exports without `export type` syntax.
findings.fix: Either: (1) Add `export type { DataSyncOrchestrator }` alias for type-only re-export, OR (2) Disable isolatedModules if not needed (rarely needed for most apps). (3) Use ts-unused-exports linter to catch dead code.

findings.title: Missing UI package dependencies: class-variance-authority and @radix-ui not in package.json
findings.trace: UI components in dashboard typically use CVA for variant management and @radix-ui for accessible components. If src/app/creator/components/ uses these, they won't be installed, causing import errors at build time.
findings.scenario: New feature: creator earnings dashboard uses @radix-ui/popover for tooltips. Developer codes component locally (with local @radix-ui/popover). Pushes to main. CI/CD runs `npm install` → missing @radix-ui/popover → build fails. Blocks other PRs.
findings.impact: Build failure, blocked deploys, developer confusion.
findings.root_cause: UI component skeleton created without full dependency list. Package.json not updated when components added.
findings.fix: Audit src/app/creator/components and src/ for all import statements from @radix-ui and class-variance-authority. Add to package.json: '@radix-ui/react-popover', 'class-variance-authority' (check which @radix-ui packages actually used).

findings.title: Schema uses ENUM types but TypeScript types are string unions with no validation layer
findings.trace: Schema enforces ENUM at DB layer. TypeScript duplicates the union. If new tier 'platinum' added to schema, TypeScript type still only knows 4 tiers. No automatic sync.
findings.scenario: DBA adds 'platinum' tier to PostgreSQL ENUM. Code not updated. API returns {tier: 'platinum'}. TypeScript parser sees 'platinum' which doesn't match CreatorTier union. No compile error (TypeScript doesn't analyze DB). At runtime, code with `switch(tier)` doesn't handle 'platinum' case.
findings.impact: Type safety illusion, unhandled enum values at runtime.
findings.root_cause: Manual type sync between schema and TypeScript. Supabase type generation tools exist (supabase gen types) but apparently not integrated.
findings.fix: Use `supabase gen types typescript --schema public > src/types/database.ts` to auto-generate types from live schema. Update tsconfig include to regenerate types on schema changes, or add pre-commit hook.

findings.title: sync-orchestrator.syncShopOrders iterates orders and fetches creator per order (N+1)
findings.code_snippet:
for (const order of result.orders) {
  const { data: creator } = await supabase
    .from('creators')
    .select('id')
    .eq('tiktok_id', order.creator_tiktok_id)
    .single();  // N+1: one query per order
findings.trace: fetchOrders() returns up to 50 orders per page. Loop over 50 orders, each does one .select().eq().single() query. At 6-hour sync interval, this could be 100-500 orders. = 100-500 DB round-trips.
findings.scenario: 7-day sync fetches 500 orders. Sequential queries at 100ms each = 50 seconds. But orchestrator has 5-min timeout (300s), so no timeout. However, Supabase connection pool is limited (~20 concurrent). With 500 sequential queries and pool contention, actual time could be 2-3 minutes, leaving little headroom for other sync jobs.
findings.impact: Slow shop order sync, less time for profile/content crawl, sync window extended.
findings.root_cause: Single order processing loop. Should batch creator lookup.
findings.fix: Fetch all unique creator_tiktok_ids from orders in one query using IN clause. Build Map of tiktok_id → creator.id. Loop over orders and look up in Map (O(1)) instead of querying.

findings.title: Reminder engine auto-detects content posted but method is unclear and may miss recent posts
findings.trace: autoDetectContentPosted() called but logic unclear. Likely queries content_tracking for matching creator_ids and updates sample_shipments.content_posted_at. But timing is unclear (does it check last 30 days? Last 7 days?)
findings.scenario: Creator posts at 3:00 AM UTC. Sync cron runs at 3:15 AM UTC. autoDetectContentPosted() checks last 7 days of content. Finds post. Updates sample_shipments.content_posted_at to NOW() (3:15 AM), not to actual posted_at from TikTok. Reminder engine never sends reminder, but creator thinks they haven't posted yet.
findings.impact: Reminder timing off, analytics show wrong timestamps.
findings.root_cause: Method implementation not shown in excerpt; requires reading full file.
findings.fix: Ensure autoDetectContentPosted() updates content_posted_at to the actual video posted_at from TikTok, not to NOW(). Add logging to track which shipments were auto-detected.

critical_blockers: PERF-001: KPI endpoint memory exhaustion at scale
STRUCT-001: content_tracking missing updated_at column breaks upsert
STRUCT-002: referral_codes/referrals missing updated_at breaks RPC
high_priority_fixes: PERF-002: N+1 in referral status updates (2,000 queries for 1,000 referrals)
CFG-001: Cron timezone mismatch (runs wrong time of day)
summary.note: Previous 2 iterations fixed parallelized runFullSync, added indexes, added limits to queries. This review found schema column mismatches, missing validation, timezone issues, and N+1 patterns in new code paths not covered by previous iterations.
